prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: One under-discussed writer is Zadie Smith, whose work is often overshadowed by her reputation as a public intellectual and essayist. Many miss the depth and complexity of her fiction, which explores themes of race, class, and identity with remarkable nuance and wit. Her novels, such as "White Teeth" and "Swing Time," are not just engaging stories but also profound meditations on the human condition, deserving of more critical attention.
